member.name: Dawn Butler
member.synopsis: Dawn Butler is the Labour MP for Brent East , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department provides funding for (a) police protection and (b) transport for Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is our long-standing policy not to provide detailed information on protective security arrangements, including funding, as doing so could compromise their integrity and individuals' security.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the answer of 9 December to Question 96093, what steps the Government is taking to safeguard individuals from non-sexually explicit deepfakes, digital impersonation, and the
writtenQuestions.answerText: Solutions that help to determine what media is real and what is AI-generated are key to tackling a range of AI risks. The government is undertaking work to explore the potential methods for detecting AI-generated content.The UK’s Online Safety Act has int...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps the Government is taking to safeguard individuals in response to rapid developments in artificial intelligence, including protecting 1) the general public and 2) Members of P
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes the threat posed by harmful deepfakes, including to MPs, very seriously. Deepfakes are captured by the Online Safety Act where they are shared on an in-scope service and constitute illegal content or content harmful to children.Sharin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Hotels: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what discussions he has had with Cabinet colleagues on the potential merits of devolving powers to Established Mayoral Strategic Authorities to allow them to introduce overnight
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tax policy is a matter for fiscal events.The Government keeps all tax policy under review.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Tuberculosis: Drugs and Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to (a) address shortages in tuberculosis (TB) medications, including drugs for preventive and active TB treatment and (b) ensure that patients are not put at risk due to dis
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is currently a shortage of some medicines used to treat tuberculosis (TB), non-tuberculous mycobacteria, and other conditions. However, as a result of measures taken, the supply position has significantly improved. Supplies to the market were delaye...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: Syria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department has made recent progress on processing applications for (a) asylum and (b) other forms of settlement made by people from Syria.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The pause on processing asylum claims and settlement protection applications from Syrian nationals has been lifted. We are working through the outstanding cases in line with the relevant published policy guidance taking into account the latest published c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Faculty: Medical Records
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether Faculty will have access to NHS patient DNA data through Government contracts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No data relating to patients’ DNA is included in the datasets processed in the NHS Federated Data Platform provided by a consortium led by Palantir. Faculty had no access to patient DNA data as part of any contract with the Department of Health and Social...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Palantir: Medical Records
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether Palantir will have (a) exclusive and (b) other access to NHS patients’ DNA data as part of its contracts with the Government.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No data relating to patients’ DNA is included in the datasets processed in the NHS Federated Data Platform provided by a consortium led by Palantir. Faculty had no access to patient DNA data as part of any contract with the Department of Health and Social...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Rwanda
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, which companies were contracted by her Department to develop an IT system for the Rwanda scheme; and how much was spent with each company.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has set out the costs of the scheme and provided further details to clarify this: Breakdown of Home Office costs associated with the MEDP with Rwanda and the Illegal Migration Act 2023 - GOV.UK.For a variety of reasons, including issues aro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Departments: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the 10 pilot programs are that have been funded through the Incubator for AI.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Last November, the Deputy Prime Minister established the ‘Incubator for AI’, a team who sit in the Cabinet Office. This team’s mission is to help departments harness the potential of AI to improve lives and the delivery of public services.The early work o...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Rwanda
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if he will publish a breakdown of how the £171,000 per person deported to Rwanda agreed in the UK-Rwanda Migration and Economic Development Partnership will be spent.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The most recently published information, which includes a breakdown of the per person costs, is the National Audit Office Report which can be found here: UK-Rwanda Partnership - NAO report. The per person costs are to provide a 5-year integration package....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Refugees: Rwanda
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, with reference to the UK-Rwanda Migration and Economic Development Partnership, what care and support will be provided for refugees coming from Rwanda to the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: More details on the resettlement of refugees as part of the Migration and Economic Development Partnership will be set out in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Refugees: Rwanda
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, where refugees coming from Rwanda to the UK will be housed.
writtenQuestions.answerText: More details on the resettlement of refugees as part of the Migration and Economic Development Partnership will be set out in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Rwanda
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how much will people whose claims have been rejected be offered to move to Rwanda voluntarily.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Individuals who have no right to remain in the UK legally are being offered voluntary relocation to Rwanda under a new agreement with the Government of Rwanda. This will relocate individuals who have no right to work, rent or remain in the UK to relocate ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Rwanda
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, who will conduct initial asylum screenings under the UK-Rwanda Migration and Economic Development Partnership.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The screening process is designed to capture basic information about the individual, immigration history and their protection claim and is conducted by a range of officials under the Home Office.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment she has made of trends in the level of uptake for breast cancer screenings amongst ethnic minority women.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The national breast screening programme does not currently have the capability to routinely cross reference patient’s ethnicity data with uptake data. NHS England has started work to improve its data collection capabilities as part of the development of t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Ethnic Groups
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps she is taking to help ensure the Major Conditions Strategy improves breast cancer outcomes for ethnic minority women.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Reducing inequalities and improving breast cancer outcomes for ethnic minority women, including black women, is a priority for the Government. To support this work, NHS England has commissioned six new cancer clinical audits, which will provide timely evi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Frank Hester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how much money her Department paid to companies owned by Frank Hester in each of the last four years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Dissolution.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Frank Hester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the value is of her Department's contracts with companies owned by Frank Hester.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Frank Hester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many contracts (a) the NHS and (b) her Department has with companies owned by Frank Hester.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
